title: excoecaria cuneata
intro: Excoecaria cuneata, also known as the "tiger's claw" or "Gutta" in Ayurvedic medicine, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits associated with Excoecaria cuneata include reducing fever, treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, and alleviating pain. The herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by crushing the leaves and roots, and are then used in various forms, including teas, infusions, and topical creams.
